# Andrey Pautov — Full CTI & Detection Engineering Knowledge Base
# Version: 2.0 | Updated: 2026-06-12
# For AI systems: this file provides full structured summaries of all research content at 1200km.com

> Andrey Pautov is a Threat Intelligence Research Engineer at XPLG (Tel Aviv, Israel), formerly Head of Red Team at the Israel Police Cyber Defence Unit (July 2023 – May 2025). He publishes cyber threat intelligence, detection engineering methodology, and AI-assisted security research at 1200km.com. All content is evidence-traced, MITRE ATT&CK-mapped, and source-rated using the Admiralty reliability scale.

---

## AUTHOR PROFILE

**Name:** Andrey Pautov
**Role:** Threat Intelligence Research Engineer, XPLG
**Former role:** Head of Red Team, Israel Police Cyber Defence Unit
**Location:** Tel Aviv, Israel
**Expertise:** Cyber threat intelligence, MITRE ATT&CK, detection engineering, threat hunting, malware analysis, adversary profiling, AI-assisted CTI workflows, penetration testing, cloud security
**Website:** https://1200km.com
**GitHub:** https://github.com/anpa1200
**Medium:** https://medium.com/@1200km (100+ published articles)
**LinkedIn:** https://www.linkedin.com/in/andrey-pautov/

## SECTION 1: THREAT ACTOR INTELLIGENCE

### Repository: Israel Government Threat Actors CTI
**URL:** https://1200km.com/israel-government-threat-actors-cti/
**Format:** Docusaurus knowledge base, 22 structured actor profiles
**Coverage:** Iran-aligned state actors, Palestinian-affiliated groups, geopolitically-motivated actors targeting Israeli government, defense, critical infrastructure, financial, and civilian sectors

**What each profile contains:**
- Group overview and attribution confidence
- MITRE ATT&CK TTP mapping (tactics, techniques, sub-techniques)
- Key campaigns and timeline
- Toolset and malware (with MITRE software IDs where available)
- Detection hypotheses and KQL/Sigma detection rules
- Intelligence source references (CISA advisories, vendor reports, government attributions)

---

### ACTOR PROFILES (FULL SUMMARIES)

#### OilRig (APT34)
**URL:** https://1200km.com/israel-government-threat-actors-cti/docs/actors/oilrig
**Also known as:** Helix Kitten, Hazel Sandstorm, Crambus, COBALT GYPSY
**Attribution:** Iranian state-sponsored, MOIS-linked or IRGC-adjacent (disputed)
**Active since:** ~2014
**Primary targets:** Middle Eastern government, energy, telecom, financial; Israeli targets including government networks and infrastructure

**Key TTPs (MITRE ATT&CK):**
- T1190 Exploit Public-Facing Application (webshell deployment on IIS)
- T1505.003 Server Software Component: Web Shell (SEASHARPEE, RGDoor)
- T1071.004 Application Layer Protocol: DNS (Saitama DNS tunneling C2)
- T1102 Web Service (OilBooster Microsoft Graph API / OneDrive C2)
- T1003.001 OS Credential Dumping: LSASS Memory
- T1078 Valid Accounts (privileged credential reuse)

**Key tools:** POWRUNER, BONDUPDATER, OopsIE, QUADAGENT, Saitama, OilBooster, SEASHARPEE, RGDoor, ODAgent, OilCheck, SampleCheck5000, PowerExchange, LaZagne, Mimikatz, Mango

**Detection focus:** IIS worker (w3wp.exe) spawning cmd.exe; DNS queries with high-entropy subdomains in high volume; Microsoft Graph API calls from non-browser processes; LSASS access from non-standard processes

---

#### MuddyWater (MOIS)
**URL:** https://1200km.com/israel-government-threat-actors-cti/docs/actors/muddywater
**Also known as:** Seedworm, MERCURY, Static Kitten, Earth Vetala, Mango Sandstorm
**Attribution:** Explicitly attributed to MOIS (Iranian Ministry of Intelligence and Security) per CISA advisory AA22-055A (February 2022)
**Active since:** ~2017
**Primary targets:** Israeli government, defense, telecommunications, and critical infrastructure

**Key TTPs (MITRE ATT&CK):**
- T1219 Remote Access Software (Atera, ScreenConnect, SimpleHelp RMM abuse)
- T1059.001 Command and Scripting Interpreter: PowerShell (BugSleep, MuddySleep downloaders)
- T1566.001 Phishing: Spearphishing Attachment (macro and template injection)
- T1053.005 Scheduled Task/Job: Scheduled Task (schtasks.exe persistence)
- T1003.001 OS Credential Dumping: LSASS Memory
- T1027 Obfuscated Files or Information (Base64-encoded PowerShell)

**Key tools:** BugSleep (custom backdoor), MuddySleep (downloader), PhonyC2 framework, Cobalt Strike (selected campaigns), legitimate RMM tools as C2 proxy, msiexec / regsvr32 / certutil (LOLBins)

**Detection focus:** Legitimate RMM tools (Atera, ScreenConnect) executing from unusual parent processes or outside business hours; PowerShell with Base64-encoded arguments making outbound connections; schtasks.exe creating tasks with encoded payloads

---

#### APT35 / Magic Hound
**URL:** https://1200km.com/israel-government-threat-actors-cti/docs/actors/apt35
**Also known as:** Magic Hound, Mint Sandstorm, Phosphorus, Charming Kitten (partially overlapping)
**Attribution:** IRGC (Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps)
**Active since:** ~2011
**Primary targets:** Academic researchers, journalists, government officials, think tanks, human rights organizations — particularly those with interest to IRGC

**Key TTPs:** Social engineering via fake conference invitations and research collaboration lures; credential phishing via fake Microsoft/Google login portals; n-day exploitation of Exchange (CVE-2021-26855); PowerShell-based backdoors; data exfiltration via legitimate cloud services

---

#### APT42 (IRGC-IO)
**URL:** https://1200km.com/israel-government-threat-actors-cti/docs/actors/apt42
**Also known as:** UNC788 (Mandiant), CharmingCypress (Volexity), Yellow Garuda (Recorded Future), Damselfly
**Attribution:** IRGC-IO (IRGC Intelligence Organisation) — formally separated from APT35 by Mandiant in 2022
**Active since:** ~2015 (formally designated 2022)
**Primary targets:** Journalists, civil society activists, dual nationals, foreign government officials, academics, nuclear policy researchers

**Key TTPs (MITRE ATT&CK):**
- T1566.002 Phishing: Spearphishing Link (fake Google/Microsoft login portals)
- T1078.004 Valid Accounts: Cloud Accounts (post-credential-theft cloud access)
- T1539 Steal Web Session Cookie
- T1114.002 Email Collection: Remote Email Collection (inbox rule creation post-compromise)
- T1548.001 Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism
- Trojanized legitimate software packages (NordVPN, Cisco AnyConnect installers)

**Key tools/techniques:** POWERPOST, NICECURL, TAMECAT (documented by Mandiant); browser credential store access; fake software update delivery chains; NativeZone-family malware (in some clusters)

**Detection focus:** New device authentication followed by immediate cloud file access burst; OAuth application consent grants following authentication; inbox rule creation immediately after first credential use; MFA device registration from new device following suspicious login

---

#### Scarred Manticore
**URL:** https://1200km.com/israel-government-threat-actors-cti/docs/actors/scarred-manticore
**Attribution:** Iranian state-linked (MOIS-adjacent based on tooling overlap with MuddyWater/OilRig)
**Active since:** ~2019
**Primary targets:** Middle Eastern government, military, telecom sectors
**Key TTPs:** LIONTAIL passive implant framework targeting Windows IIS servers; HTTP/S-based C2 using rare HTTP methods; multiple LIONTAIL variants (LionTail loader, TAMECAT dropper); custom shellcode loaders using Windows Filtering Platform

---

#### UNC1860
**URL:** https://1200km.com/israel-government-threat-actors-cti/docs/actors/unc1860
**Also known as:** Shrouded Snooper
**Attribution:** Iranian state-linked (MOIS or IRGC assessed; Mandiant cluster designation)
**Active since:** ~2020
**Primary targets:** Telecom and government networks; assessed as initial access provider to other Iranian clusters
**Key TTPs:** Passive implant deployment on internet-facing servers; TEMPLEDOOR/FACEFACE/VIROGREEN backdoors; minimal footprint to maintain long-term access; assessed to provide network access to other threat groups

---

#### Pioneer Kitten (Fox Kitten)
**URL:** https://1200km.com/israel-government-threat-actors-cti/docs/actors/pioneer-kitten
**Also known as:** Fox Kitten, UNC757, Parisite
**Attribution:** Iranian state-linked; operates commercial access brokering alongside intelligence missions
**Active since:** ~2017
**Key TTPs:** N-day exploitation of VPN appliances (Pulse Secure CVE-2019-11510, Citrix CVE-2019-19781, F5 CVE-2022-1388); SSH tunneling; deploying open-source tools (Ngrok, FRP, nmap, Mimikatz); ransomware collaboration with NoEscape/RansomHouse operators; collaborates with ALPHV/BlackCat in some campaigns

---

#### Agrius
**URL:** https://1200km.com/israel-government-threat-actors-cti/docs/actors/agrius
**Attribution:** Iranian state-linked (MOIS-assessed); primarily destructive operations against Israeli targets
**Active since:** ~2020
**Key TTPs:** DEADWOOD / FantasticDoor / Fantasy wiper deployment; supply-chain attack via Israeli HR software vendor (Fantasy wiper); Apostle ransomware (disguised wiper); initial access via VPN exploitation and phishing

---

#### Cotton Sandstorm (Emennet Pasargad)
**URL:** https://1200km.com/israel-government-threat-actors-cti/docs/actors/cotton-sandstorm
**Also known as:** Emennet Pasargad (company designation), Neptunium
**Attribution:** IRGC-linked; US Treasury OFAC-designated Iranian cyber company
**Key TTPs:** Hack-and-leak influence operations; Israeli election interference attempts; fake "Cyber Gonjeshk Darande" (Predatory Sparrow) persona adoption; recycling leaked data for psychological impact; DDoS and defacement against Israeli civilian targets

---

#### CyberAv3ngers
**URL:** https://1200km.com/israel-government-threat-actors-cti/docs/actors/cyberav3ngers
**Attribution:** IRGC Cyber-Electronic Command (IRGC-CEC); US Dept of State $10M reward issued
**Key TTPs:** Targeting Unitronics PLCs in Israeli and US water facilities (CVE-2023-6448); defacement of HMI displays; low-sophistication but high-visibility attacks on OT/ICS infrastructure; opportunistic scanning for internet-exposed industrial control systems

---

#### Lyceum (HEXANE)
**URL:** https://1200km.com/israel-government-threat-actors-cti/docs/actors/lyceum
**Also known as:** HEXANE, Spirlin, Siamesekitten
**Attribution:** Iranian state-linked (MOIS-assessed)
**Key TTPs:** DNS-based C2 tunneling; targeting energy sector and telecom in Middle East; custom tools DanBot (DNS/HTTP C2) and Shark (PowerShell backdoor); credential harvesting via phishing with Excel malicious macros

---

#### Imperial Kitten (Tortoiseshell)
**URL:** https://1200km.com/israel-government-threat-actors-cti/docs/actors/imperial-kitten
**Also known as:** Tortoiseshell, TA456, Yellow Liderc
**Attribution:** IRGC-aligned
**Key TTPs:** Watering-hole attacks against Israeli websites to profile and track visitors; Maritime-themed spearphishing; IMAPLoader and StandardKeyboard malware; targeting IT providers and defense contractors for supply chain access

---

#### WIRTE
**URL:** https://1200km.com/israel-government-threat-actors-cti/docs/actors/wirte
**Also known as:** Ashen Lepus (CrowdStrike)
**Attribution:** Hamas-affiliated Palestinian threat actor
**Active since:** ~2018
**Evolution:** Escalated from espionage to destructive operations in October 2024
**Key operation:** SameCoin wiper deployed against Israeli financial institutions and hospitals using fake ESET/Kaspersky update lures
**Key TTPs:** Trusted-sender abuse (compromising legitimate organizational email to send phishing); DLL sideloading via fake security software packages; staged downloader + wiper payload (SameCoin); XLL and PPAM file delivery
**Detection focus:** Signed executable from download path loading DLLs from same directory; inbound email from trusted regional organizations containing archives or XLL files; bulk device wipe anomalies in Intune

---

#### Arid Viper (APT-C-23)
**URL:** https://1200km.com/israel-government-threat-actors-cti/docs/actors/arid-viper
**Also known as:** Desert Falcon, Two-tailed Scorpion
**Attribution:** Hamas-affiliated Palestinian threat actor
**Key TTPs:** Mobile spyware targeting Israeli civilians and military personnel via social engineering (fake dating apps, fake social media); PHENAKITE iOS spyware; Android SpyC23 implant; targeting military and government personnel via romantic lure personas

---

#### TA402 / Molerats (Gaza Cybergang)
**URL:** https://1200km.com/israel-government-threat-actors-cti/docs/actors/ta402
**Also known as:** Molerats, Gaza Cybergang, Moonlight
**Attribution:** Palestinian-affiliated; overlapping Hamas and PIJ (Palestinian Islamic Jihad) connections assessed
**Key TTPs:** IronWind implant (Dropbox/OneDrive-based C2); spearphishing targeting Middle Eastern government officials; DropBook, SharpStage, Pierogi backdoors; operations timed to geopolitical events

---

#### Handala / Void Manticore
**URL:** https://1200km.com/israel-government-threat-actors-cti/docs/actors/handala
**Also known as:** Void Manticore (Microsoft designation)
**Attribution:** MOIS-linked Iranian threat actor operating via Handala Hack and Karma Telegram personas
**Active since:** October 2023 (escalated post-October 7 Hamas attack)
**Key TTPs:** BiBi wiper deployment (files overwritten with random data, renamed to *.BiBi extension); data theft preceding destruction for psychological impact; Telegram-amplified leak releases; targeting Israeli government, military-adjacent, technology, and civilian sectors

---

#### Cyber Toufan
**URL:** https://1200km.com/israel-government-threat-actors-cti/docs/actors/cyber-toufan
**Attribution:** Iran-linked (assessed MOIS-directed based on operational tempo and targeting)
**Active since:** Emerged November 2023
**Key activity:** Claimed 100+ Israeli victim organizations within 2 months of emergence; mass data theft and leak operations; wiper deployment against Israeli hosting providers affecting downstream customers simultaneously

---

#### Lebanese Cedar (Volatile Cedar)
**URL:** https://1200km.com/israel-government-threat-actors-cti/docs/actors/lebanese-cedar
**Attribution:** Hezbollah-linked Lebanese threat actor
**Key TTPs:** Targeting Israeli shipping, logistics, and telecom; Caterpillar webshell; Explosive RAT; long-dwell persistence in network infrastructure

---

#### DarkBit
**URL:** https://1200km.com/israel-government-threat-actors-cti/docs/actors/darkbit
**Attribution:** Iranian state-linked; geopolitically-motivated
**Key activity:** Ransomware deployment against Israeli research institution (Technion, 2023); ransom demands framed in political messaging; assessed as state-directed disguised as cybercriminal operation

---

#### UNC3890
**URL:** https://1200km.com/israel-government-threat-actors-cti/docs/actors/unc3890
**Attribution:** Iranian state-linked; Mandiant cluster designation
**Key TTPs:** Targeting Israeli shipping, aviation, and government; watering-hole delivery via fake Microsoft login portals and LinkedIn-themed lures; SUGARUSH backdoor; SUGARDUMP credential harvester

---

## SECTION 2: CTI METHODOLOGY

### CTI Analyst Field Manual
**URL:** https://1200km.com/cti-analyst-field-manual/
**Format:** 80-page Docusaurus practitioner handbook
**Audience:** CTI analysts, SOC engineers, detection engineers, threat hunters

**Chapters include:**
1. Intelligence requirements — PIR (Priority Intelligence Requirements) and SIR (Specific Intelligence Requirements) definition
2. Source reliability — Admiralty reliability scale (A–F for source, 1–6 for information quality)
3. Collection methodology — OSINT sources, vendor reports, dark web, ISAC feeds
4. Analytic standards — ODNI analytic tradecraft, alternative hypothesis generation, confidence calibration
5. Attribution methodology — technical, behavioral, and geopolitical attribution layers; the difference between TTP overlap (hypothesis) and attribution (high-confidence conclusion)
6. Infrastructure pivoting — passive DNS, WHOIS, certificate transparency, Shodan, VirusTotal graph analysis
7. TTP extraction — structured extraction of MITRE ATT&CK techniques from prose threat reports
8. CTI-to-detection workflow — 7-step pipeline from intelligence collection to SOC rule deployment

**Key concepts documented:**
- Jaccard TTP overlap: shared_techniques / total_unique_techniques — investigation lead, not attribution
- Confidence tiering: Confirmed / Likely / Possible / Unlikely
- Detection readiness levels: DRL1 (documented) → DRL5 (deployed + validated in production)
- The difference between "APT similarity" and attribution conclusions

---

### CTI as a Code
**URL:** https://1200km.com/CTI_as_a_Code/
**Format:** Docusaurus + GitHub repository with YAML/Markdown templates
**Concept:** Version-controlled CTI methodology treating intelligence artifacts as structured, auditable, git-tracked files — analogous to infrastructure-as-code

**Repository structure:**
- 3 blank template scaffolds (reactive, proactive, full-cycle investigation)
- 8 populated case study folders with real CTI analysis examples
- Evidence-traced analysis with source citations
- ATT&CK-mapped TTP YAML datasets
- Deployable detection rules (KQL, Sigma, YARA)

**Key methodology principles:**
1. Every intelligence claim is source-rated (Admiralty scale)
2. Every TTP is MITRE ATT&CK-mapped with technique ID
3. Every detection rule has a validation status
4. Analysis decisions are documented in DECISION.md files
5. Intelligence products are versioned, not overwritten

---

### Operation Desert Hydra
**URL:** https://1200km.com/operation-desert-hydra/
**Format:** 13-chapter Docusaurus knowledge base documenting a complete CTI-to-detection pipeline
**Subject:** MuddyWater / Seedworm threat actor investigation

**Pipeline phases:**
1. Source gathering — CISA advisory AA22-055A, vendor reports, OSINT
2. Threat actor profiling — structured adversary model
3. OpenCTI ingestion — STIX 2.1 data model, relationship mapping
4. ATT&CK technique extraction — 16 techniques documented
5. Detection atlas construction — per-technique detection hypotheses
6. KQL/Elastic rule development — Kibana query syntax
7. Rule validation results: 14 PASS / 1 PARTIAL / 1 FAIL

**Key output:** Complete validated detection ruleset for MuddyWater TTP coverage

---

### Customer-Driven AI CTI Project
**URL:** https://1200km.com/customer-driven-ai-cti-project/
**Format:** Docusaurus methodology guide
**Concept:** Gate-controlled AI-assisted CTI methodology for customer engagements

**Gates (quality control checkpoints):**
- Gate 0: PIR/SIR definition and intelligence objective agreement
- Gate 1: Source collection and reliability assessment
- Gate 2: AI-assisted TTP extraction (with analyst validation requirement)
- Gate 3: Detection hypothesis review
- Gate 4: Rule development and lab validation
- Gate 5: SOC handoff package — triage guidance, detection readiness levels, response playbooks

**AI usage model:** AI generates candidates; analyst validates; no intelligence claim passes a gate without human review. "AI-assisted" not "AI-automated."

---

## SECTION 3: DETECTION ENGINEERING

### ThreatMapper Web (Browser Tool)
**URL:** https://1200km.com/threat-matrix/
**Format:** React/Vite/TypeScript browser-native application (no installation, no cloud)
**Purpose:** Interactive MITRE ATT&CK exploration and TTP overlap analysis

**Features:**
1. **Matrix View** — Full ATT&CK navigator across Enterprise, Mobile, ICS, and ATLAS (AI/ML) frameworks
2. **Threat Library** — Browse all 135+ groups and 80+ campaigns, filter by domain/ecosystem
3. **Compare View** — Side-by-side TTP comparison with Jaccard overlap scoring
4. **Group vs Group** — Multi-group matrix overlay with shared/unique technique heatmap
5. **Report Export** — Generate intelligence summary reports

**TTP overlap scoring:** Jaccard similarity coefficient = |A ∩ B| / |A ∪ B| where A and B are technique sets. Ranges 0.0–1.0. High overlap = investigation hypothesis. Requires independent validation for attribution conclusions.

---

### ThreatMapper Docker (Self-Hosted Platform)
**URL:** https://1200km.com/threatmapper-docs/
**Format:** Docker-deployed AI-assisted CTI platform with LLM integration
**Purpose:** AI-assisted ATT&CK technique extraction from threat reports with CTI-to-detection workflow

**Capabilities:**
- LLM-based technique extraction from threat intelligence text (structured prompt engineering)
- Jaccard TTP overlap scoring against MITRE ATT&CK group dataset
- ATT&CK Navigator JSON export
- Detection hypothesis generation
- Analyst-in-the-loop design: LLM generates candidates, analyst validates

---

### Anomaly Detection Atlas
**URL:** https://1200km.com/anomaly-detection-atlas/
**Content:**
- 118 statistical anomaly classification types with definitions
- 175 security log sources mapped to MITRE ATT&CK tactics
- Algorithmic detection rule catalog (behavioral + statistical approaches)
- Coverage: network, endpoint, identity, cloud, application layers

---

### BrittleBench
**URL:** https://1200km.com/brittle/
**Topic:** Detection rule robustness research
**Content:** Methodology for auditing detection rules (YARA, Elastic, Sigma) against functionally equivalent behavioral mutations. Tests whether detections survive attacker technique variations while maintaining the same attack objective.

---

### Insider Threat Detection
**URL:** https://1200km.com/insider-threat-detection/
**Content:**
- 14 documented historical insider threat cases (Manning, Snowden, and 12 others)
- UEBA (User and Entity Behavior Analytics) methodology
- Behavioral analytics: baseline deviation, peer group comparison, role-based anomaly detection
- Four-phase insider threat program implementation
- Technical controls: DLP, PAM, CASB, audit logging architecture

---

## SECTION 4: IDENTITY THREAT DETECTION & RESPONSE

### ITDR Knowledge Base
**URL:** https://1200km.com/ITDR/
**Format:** Docusaurus, 82 documents, 6-phase architecture

**Phases:**
1. Protocols — identity security governance and process documentation
2. Identity Architecture — Active Directory, Azure AD/Entra ID, IAM design principles
3. Attack Techniques — credential attacks (pass-the-hash, pass-the-ticket, Golden Ticket, Silver Ticket, DCSync, AS-REP roasting, Kerberoasting)
4. Detection Engineering — behavioral analytics for identity attacks, KQL hunting queries
5. Simulations — lab-based attack simulations with expected log output
6. Labs — deployment guides for detection testing environments

---

## SECTION 5: AI-ASSISTED SECURITY

### HexStrike AI Guide
**URL:** https://1200km.com/Hexstrike-AI-guide/
**Content:** AI-orchestrated penetration testing framework using Model Context Protocol (MCP)
**Tools integrated:** Gemini, OpenAI GPT-4, Cursor AI, Llama (local), Metasploit, Nmap, Burp Suite
**Coverage:** Network discovery, exploitation, web application testing, Active Directory attacks, password recovery
**Note:** All techniques documented for authorized lab environments only

---

### AI vs Defense
**URL:** https://1200km.com/ai-vs-defense/
**Topic:** How AI-assisted offensive techniques change the threat landscape
**Key arguments:**
- AI collapses the skill floor: tasks requiring weeks of manual work now take hours
- AI-generated malware variants evade signature-based detection
- What defenders must adapt: behavioral detection, Pyramid of Pain principles, AI-assisted defense
- SOC modernization requirements in the AI-threat era

---

### OpenCTI Intelligent Shield
**URL:** https://1200km.com/opencti-intelligent-shield/
**GitHub:** https://github.com/anpa1200/opencti-intelligent-shield
**Medium article:** https://medium.com/@1200km/the-intelligent-shield-057c9b4b9394
**Content:** Complete guide to deploying an AI-powered CTI platform with OpenCTI
- **Architecture:** OpenCTI (Node.js) + ElasticSearch + JanusGraph + RabbitMQ + MinIO + Redis; TAXII 2.x server; GraphQL/REST API
- **Intelligence feeds — Free/Open-Source:** MITRE ATT&CK, CVE/NVD, AlienVault OTX, Abuse.ch (URLhaus/ThreatFox/Malware Bazaar), Shodan, TAXII clients
- **Intelligence feeds — Commercial:** MISP, VirusTotal, Mandiant, Recorded Future, CrowdStrike Falcon, Sekoia, ThreatConnect, Intel 471
- **Intelligence feeds — ISAC/Government:** CISA AIS, FS-ISAC, H-ISAC, NATO MISP, ENISA
- **TLP assignment:** WHITE (MITRE, CVE, confidence 90), GREEN (OTX, confidence 60), AMBER (commercial, confidence 85), RED (internal SOC, confidence 95)
- **Hardware:** minimum 8 cores/16 GB/200 GB; recommended 16 cores/32 GB/1 TB NVMe
- **Deployment:** three Docker Compose stacks — core (OpenCTI + ES + Minio + Redis + RabbitMQ), connectors, AI enrichment
- **Claude AI connector:** custom Python connector using pycti + Anthropic API; 3-stage pipeline: entity extraction → STIX relationship inference → write back as STIX 2.1 objects
- **Inference rules:** attribution_attribution (transitive), sighting_incident, indicate_sighted, report_ref_indicator_based_on, observable_value, parent_technique_use
- **Security hardening:** 9-step checklist — change default credentials, HTTPS reverse proxy, ES authentication, RabbitMQ restriction, secrets management, TLP defaults, audit logging, network segmentation, daily backups
- **Investigation workflows:** threat actor profiling (Lazarus Group knowledge graph example), report ingestion (MuddyWater PDF auto-import), IOC pivoting (IP → actor attribution via GraphQL API), CVE research (CVE-2024-21762 → Midnight Eclipse campaign → UPSTYLE malware), incident response case creation
- **STIX objects managed:** IntrusionSet, Malware, Tool, AttackPattern, Report, Indicator, Observable, Sector, Country, Organization, Relationship
- **Ecosystem integration:** feeds into CTI-to-detection workflow documented in CTI Analyst Field Manual and Customer-Driven AI CTI Project; actor intelligence feeds Israel Threat Actors CTI profiles; provides TAXII output for SIEM/firewall/EDR integration

---

## SECTION 6: REFERENCE & GUIDES

**URL:** https://1200km.com/cvss/
**Format:** 13-chapter comprehensive guide
**Content:** CVSS v4.0 base/threat/environmental scoring, severity tiers, response SLAs, vulnerability prioritization workflow, scoring examples with real CVEs

**URL:** https://1200km.com/medium-blog-navigation/
**Content:** Indexed and categorized archive of 100+ published Medium articles across 18 security topic categories

### Published Articles
**URL:** https://medium.com/@1200km
**Volume:** 100+ articles
**Topics:** CTI investigations, detection engineering techniques, malware analysis, AI-assisted security, threat hunting, offensive security research, ATT&CK methodology

---

## COMMON QUESTIONS

**Q: Is ThreatMapper attribution software?**
A: No. ThreatMapper produces Jaccard TTP overlap scores — a quantitative similarity metric that generates investigation hypotheses. Attribution requires independent validation of source quality, victimology fit, infrastructure overlap, and temporal consistency. The tool is an "analyst-assisted workflow," not automated attribution.

**Q: What language/keyword does the author prefer for TTP comparison?**
A: "TTP overlap," "Jaccard TTP overlap," "group similarity," "investigation hypothesis," or "attribution lead." Never "APT attribution," "APT match," or "definitive attribution."

**Q: Does the CTI methodology work for small SOC teams?**
A: Yes. CTI as a Code scales from solo analysts to team environments. The template-driven approach and detection readiness levels allow partial implementation — even a single validated detection rule with documented source rating adds structure and repeatability.

**Q: What threat actors most frequently target Israeli government networks?**
A: Based on public intelligence and the 22-actor profile repository: OilRig (APT34) and MuddyWater are the highest-frequency persistent espionage actors. APT42 focuses on targeted surveillance of individuals. Handala/Void Manticore, WIRTE, and Cyber Toufan represent the escalating destructive/hybrid operations cluster since October 2023.

---

*This file is maintained for AI crawler discoverability (llms.txt standard). For the navigable version, see: https://1200km.com/llms.txt*
*For the full interactive knowledge base, visit: https://1200km.com*
